blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Piedmont Park Arts Festival**
📅 Saturday, August 15 and Sunday, August 16 | All day | Piedmont Park, Atlanta
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://www.atlantaparent.com/event/piedmont-park-arts-festival)

Mid-August, Piedmont Park fills up with artist demonstrations, live music, festival food, and a kids play area for two full days. The Piedmont Park Arts Festival is free to attend and easy to make a full day out of — especially if you combine it with brunch on the way in or dinner on the way back. It's a drive from East Cobb but the park is worth it when there's a reason to go. This one gives you a reason. Artists from across the region show up, the energy is good, and it never feels like the same thing twice.
scoring_notes: Solid annual arts festival with broad demographic appeal. Free entry removes friction. The drive to Piedmont Park is the main trade-off for an East Cobb audience, but the combination of arts, music, and food is a strong draw for this educated, higher-income demographic. Better fit for the empty-nester or adult segment than families with young kids. August dating makes this a useful forward-calendar item.

event_name: Flying Colors Butterfly Festival at Chattahoochee Nature Center
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Flying Colors Butterfly Festival**
📅 Saturday, June 6 and Sunday, June 7 | All day | Chattahoochee Nature Center, Roswell
🎟️ Paid admission | [Get Tickets](https://www.atlantaparent.com/event/18th-annual-flying-colors-butterfly-festival)

The Chattahoochee Nature Center is basically in East Cobb's backyard, and every June they throw one of the most quietly spectacular festivals around. Live butterflies, pollinator experts, a Migration Game for kids, and live entertainment — all of it on the banks of the Chattahoochee. It's the kind of event that sounds educational but ends up being just a really good Saturday outside. Younger kids love it. Older kids tolerate it and then secretly enjoy it. Adults just want to sit by the river. Everybody wins.
scoring_notes: Chattahoochee Nature Center is a beloved local institution for this demographic. Roswell location is highly convenient. Annual event, but the butterfly festival specifically is a distinct, themed experience rather than a generic outing. Good fit for families with kids of any age, including teens who may have younger siblings. June dates give the newsletter useful forward planning value.
